channel.title: NPR Programs: All Things Considered
channel.description: All Things Considered hosts Ailsa Chang, Mary Louise Kelly, Juana Summers and Scott Detrow present the program's trademark mix of news, interviews, commentaries, reviews, and offbeat features 7 days a week.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Some states with legal gambling are concerned about losing a cut to new technology
item.description: With college hoops in the air, lawmakers in New Hampshire and other states worry their cut of legal gambling revenue could shrink as bettors turn to the latest tech platform, prediction markets.
item.content:encoded: With college hoops in the air, lawmakers in New Hampshire and other states worry their cut of legal gambling revenue could shrink as bettors turn to the latest tech platform, prediction markets.
item.dc:creator: Todd Bookman

item.title: Supreme Court hears arguments around Trump's order limiting birthright citizenship
item.description: The Supreme Court heard arguments Wednesday on birthright citizenship at a key political moment for President Trump.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court heard arguments Wednesday on birthright citizenship at a key political moment for President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: 25 years ago today, a mid-air collision sparked an 11-day U.S.–China standoff
item.description: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.content:encoded: On April 1, 2001, a midair collision between an American aircraft and a Chinese plane sparked an 11-day diplomatic crisis — and the two countries still tell very different stories.
item.dc:creator: Scott Tong

item.title: Who is held accountable if a war crime is committed in Iran?
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with international law expert Gabor Rona about attacks on civilian infrastructure in the Iran conflict and how they may constitute war crimes.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson

item.title: Christian therapists and LGBTQ people of faith respond to Supreme Court ruling
item.description: The Supreme Court's decision outlawing bans on conversion therapy will change the legal situation in many states. An evangelical Christian therapist and LGBTQ activist share how people are responding.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court's decision outlawing bans on conversion therapy will change the legal situation in many states. An evangelical Christian therapist and LGBTQ activist share how people are responding.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: How long the Strait of Hormuz stays closed will have major economic implications
item.description: What are the implications for the oil market and the broader global economy if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed for months?
item.content:encoded: What are the implications for the oil market and the broader global economy if the Strait of Hormuz remains largely closed for months?
item.dc:creator: Camila Domonoske

item.title: Iran war adds more expense for profit-starved U.S. grain farmers
item.description: New federal data shows American farmers are planting millions fewer acres of corn and wheat in response to much higher fertilizer prices since the U.S. attacked Iran.
item.content:encoded: New federal data shows American farmers are planting millions fewer acres of corn and wheat in response to much higher fertilizer prices since the U.S. attacked Iran.
item.dc:creator: Kirk Siegler

item.title: NASA prepares to send four Artemis II astronauts on a lunar flyby
item.description: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.content:encoded: NASA is preparing to launch four people on a trip around the moon and back. It's the first time in more than a half-century humans are set to venture around the moon.
item.dc:creator: Nell Greenfieldboyce

item.title: Amidst conflicting signals, Trump addresses nation on Iran war
item.description: Amidst conflicting signals from the White House on the war in Iran, the president addresses the nation Wednesday on the conflict.
item.content:encoded: Amidst conflicting signals from the White House on the war in Iran, the president addresses the nation Wednesday on the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram

item.title: Study links falling for corporate buzzwords with poor decision-making
item.description: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Shane Littrell of Cornell University, whose new study concludes that those who buy into corporate jargon may actually be worse at their jobs.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Scott Detrow speaks with Shane Littrell of Cornell University, whose new study concludes that those who buy into corporate jargon may actually be worse at their jobs.
item.dc:creator: Henry Larson
